plan:
- Flight Control: Pixhawk 6C (or similar) with ArduPilot firmware, GNSS (e.g., ZED-F9P) for centimeter-level positioning, sonic/laser altimeter for terrain following.
- Sensor Payload: Two triaxial fluxgate magnetometers (e.g., HWT3100-485/FGM3D/75), Arduino Mega (or similar) microcontroller for data acquisition.
- Power & Propulsion: 4S 5000mAh LiPo battery, ESCs, motors, propellers, power distribution board with regulated 5V/12V outputs.
- Structural: Quadcopter frame (carbon fiber/plastic, DJI F-450 inspired) up to 1.5kg MTOW, non-magnetic retractable boom (30-50 cm).
- Communication & Data: 433/915 MHz SiK radio telemetry, onboard SD card for logging, optional Raspberry Pi 4/Zero for processing/logging.
- Software: Arduino code for sensor reading, QGroundControl/Mission Planner for flight control, custom ground station software for mapping.

nodes.name: Flight Controller
nodes.description: Primary flight control unit based on STM32H753, includes integrated IMU (ICM-20689) and barometer (MS5611) for stable flight and navigation.

nodes.name: Payload Data Acquisition MCU
nodes.description: Microcontroller board based on the ATmega2560, suitable for projects requiring many I/O pins.

nodes.name: Centimeter-Level GPS Module
nodes.description: Provides precise positioning data for autonomous navigation.

nodes.name: Precision Laser Altimeter
nodes.description: LiDAR for accurate terrain following and altitude hold.

nodes.name: Main LiPo Battery
nodes.description: 4S 5000mAh LiPo battery for main power supply.

nodes.name: Motor Front Left
nodes.description: Brushless motor for propeller rotation.

nodes.name: Motor Front Right
nodes.description: Brushless motor for propeller rotation.

nodes.name: Motor Rear Left
nodes.description: Brushless motor for propeller rotation.

nodes.name: Motor Rear Right
nodes.description: Brushless motor for propeller rotation.

nodes.name: ESC Front Left
nodes.description: Electronic Speed Controller for the left brushless DC motor.

nodes.name: ESC Front Right
nodes.description: Electronic Speed Controller for the left brushless DC motor.

nodes.name: ESC Rear Left
nodes.description: Electronic Speed Controller for the left brushless DC motor.

nodes.name: ESC Rear Right
nodes.description: Electronic Speed Controller for the left brushless DC motor.

nodes.name: Telemetry Radio Module
nodes.productName: Holybro SiK Telemetry Radio V3 (433MHz or 915MHz)

nodes.name: Main Frame Bottom Plate
nodes.description: Bottom plate of the quadcopter frame, typically holding the power distribution board and providing mounting points for arms and landing gear.
nodes.productName: DJI F450 Quadcopter Frame Carbon Fiber Plate

nodes.name: Main Frame Top Plate
nodes.description: Top plate of the quadcopter frame, providing mounting for flight controller, battery, and other electronics.
nodes.productName: DJI F450 Quadcopter Frame Carbon Fiber Plate

nodes.name: Frame Arm Front Left
nodes.description: Front left arm of the quadcopter frame, supporting the motor and ESC.

nodes.name: Frame Arm Front Right
nodes.description: Front right arm of the quadcopter frame, supporting the motor and ESC.

nodes.name: Frame Arm Rear Left
nodes.description: Rear left arm of the quadcopter frame, supporting the motor and ESC.

nodes.name: Frame Arm Rear Right
nodes.description: Rear right arm of the quadcopter frame, supporting the motor and ESC.

nodes.name: Landing Skid Set
nodes.description: Standard landing gear skids to provide stable ground clearance for the drone.

nodes.name: Propeller Front Left
nodes.description: Self-tightening plastic propeller for the front left motor, rotating clockwise.

nodes.name: Propeller Front Right
nodes.description: Self-tightening plastic propeller for the front right motor, rotating counter-clockwise.

nodes.name: Propeller Rear Left
nodes.description: Self-tightening plastic propeller for the rear left motor, rotating counter-clockwise.

nodes.name: Propeller Rear Right
nodes.description: Self-tightening plastic propeller for the rear right motor, rotating clockwise.

nodes.name: Collapsible Sensor Boom
nodes.description: Lightweight, non-magnetic carbon fiber telescopic pole, 30-50cm extended, to minimize magnetic interference from the drone.

nodes.name: Boom Retraction Mechanism
nodes.description: 3D-printed mechanism to attach and allow retraction/extension of the sensor boom, operated by a micro servo.

nodes.name: Z-Axis Magnetometer Mount
nodes.description: 3D-printed mount for the vertical magnetometer, ensuring Z-axis orientation and secure attachment to the sensor boom.

nodes.name: X-Axis Magnetometer Mount
nodes.description: 3D-printed mount for the horizontal magnetometer, ensuring X-axis orientation and secure attachment to the sensor boom, next to the Z-axis mag.

nodes.name: Laser Altimeter Mount
nodes.description: 3D-printed bracket to securely attach the laser altimeter to the sensor boom, ensuring an unobstructed downward view.

nodes.name: Flight Controller Mount
nodes.description: Vibration-dampening mount for the Pixhawk flight controller, isolating it from frame vibrations for better IMU performance.

nodes.name: Payload MCU Mount
nodes.description: 3D-printed tray or standoffs to mount the Arduino Mega payload microcontroller securely on the frame's top plate.

nodes.name: GNSS Module Mast
nodes.description: Tall 3D-printed mast for elevating the GNSS module above the main frame to improve signal reception.

nodes.name: GNSS Module Top Mount
nodes.description: Small 3D-printed platform to mount the GNSS module securely on top of the GNSS mast.

nodes.name: Telemetry Radio Mount
nodes.description: 3D-printed enclosure or bracket for the telemetry radio module, typically mounted on a frame arm or side.

nodes.name: LiPo Battery Strap
nodes.description: Velcro strap for securing the main LiPo battery to the drone's top plate.

nodes.name: M3 Nylon Standoff
nodes.description: Nylon standoffs used for mounting PCBs and components to avoid short circuits and provide spacing.

nodes.name: M3 Nylon Screw
nodes.description: Nylon screws used with standoffs for mounting components, non-magnetic.

nodes.name: M3 Nylon Nut
nodes.description: Nylon nuts for securing nylon screws and standoffs, non-magnetic.

nodes.name: Cable Ties
nodes.description: Assorted small plastic cable ties for wire management and securing lightweight components.

nodes.name: ESC Front Left Mount
nodes.description: Per-position bracket for the ESC Front Left with M3 mounting holes matched to its bolt pattern. Rated for the actuator's continuous torque/load class.

nodes.name: ESC Front Right Mount
nodes.description: Per-position bracket for the ESC Front Right with M3 mounting holes matched to its bolt pattern. Rated for the actuator's continuous torque/load class.

nodes.name: ESC Rear Left Mount
nodes.description: Per-position bracket for the ESC Rear Left with M3 mounting holes matched to its bolt pattern. Rated for the actuator's continuous torque/load class.

nodes.name: ESC Rear Right Mount
nodes.description: Per-position bracket for the ESC Rear Right with M3 mounting holes matched to its bolt pattern. Rated for the actuator's continuous torque/load class.

originalPrompt:
Design a drone for magnetometer-based landmine/UXO detection based on the scientific paper "Intelligent, Automated, Rapid, and Safe Landmine, IED and UXO Detection Using Maggy" (IEEE Access, 2024, DOI: 10.1109/ACCESS.2024.3493013).

**Drone platform:**
- Type: Quadcopter, inspired by DJI F-450 frame
- Maximum takeoff weight: 1.5 kg (including payload)
- Operational weight with sensors and battery: ~880 g
- Flight speed: 1 m/s for detailed scanning (can be increased to 2–5 m/s)
- Flight altitude: 0.5–1.0 m above ground level (AGL) with terrain following capability using altimeter
- Endurance: Minimum 15 minutes (improve from original 4.5 min by using larger battery, e.g., 4S 5000mAh LiPo)
- Frame material: Carbon fiber or plastic (non-magnetic to reduce interference)

**Sensor payload:**
- Primary sensor: Two triaxial fluxgate magnetometers (e.g., HWT3100-485 or FGM3D/75)
  - Magnetic field range: ±800 µT
  - Sensitivity: 13 nT/LSB
  - Sampling rate: 10–200 Hz (configurable)
  - Output: Serial 485 / Modbus / Bluetooth
- Mounting: One magnetometer oriented to measure Z-axis (vertical), another for X-axis (horizontal). Place sensors below the drone on a non-magnetic retractable boom (30–50 cm) to minimize interference from motors and ESCs.
- Secondary sensor (optional): Altimeter (sonic/laser) for precision height hold under 1 m.
- GPS: Internal + external GNSS (e.g., ZED-F9P) for centimeter-level positioning.

**Processing unit:**
- Low-power microcontroller (Arduino Mega or similar) for real-time sensor data acquisition at 200 Hz.
- Optional onboard computer (Raspberry Pi 4 or Zero) for data processing, logging, and communication with ground station.

**Signal processing and detection:**
- Convert raw data to magnetic field (µT) using: MF = (raw × sensitivity)/1000 (sensitivity = 13 nT/LSB).
- Compute total field magnitude: MF_XYZ = sqrt(MFX² + MFY² + MFZ²).
- Compute heading: heading = atan2(MFY, MFX) × (180/π).
- Apply threshold classification (user-selectable) to identify anomalies above background magnetic field.
- Implement real-time filtering (e.g., moving average or median filter) to reduce noise.

**Flight control and navigation:**
- Flight controller: Pixhawk 6C or equivalent with ArduPilot firmware.
- Modes: Position hold, altitude hold, waypoint navigation, terrain following.
- Ground control station: QGroundControl or Mission Planner running on laptop/tablet.

**Power system:**
- Battery: 4S LiPo, 5000 mAh (minimum) to achieve 15–20 min flight time.
- Power distribution board with regulated 5V and 12V outputs for sensors and Arduino.

**Data telemetry:**
- Telemetry link: 433/915 MHz SiK radio for live data streaming to ground station.
- Data logging: Onboard SD card (2 GB minimum) for storing raw MF data with GPS timestamps.

**Software:**
- Arduino code for sensor reading (Python or C++).
- Ground station software for real-time magnetic field mapping (color-coded anomalies).
- Optional: Integration with deep learning model (YOLOv5 as in NPA study) for automated target recognition.

**References:**
- Kuru et al., "Intelligent, Automated, Rapid, and Safe Landmine, IED and UXO Detection Using Maggy", IEEE Access, 2024.
- Garcia-Fernandez et al., "Drone-mounted Magnetometer Interference Removal", arXiv:2510.01417, 2025.

Deliverables required from Blueprints.am:
1. Complete BOM (Bill of Materials) with component specifications and estimated costs.
2. Circuit schematic diagram for sensor integration (Arduino + magnetometers + GPS + telemetry).
3. 3D CAD model of the drone frame with sensor boom mounting.
4. Step-by-step assembly and calibration instructions.
5. Flight parameters (PIDs, waypoint planning for grid scanning).

subSteps.title: 3D print all custom mounts and mechanisms using specified materials and settings
Deburr and clean all 3D printed parts, ensuring smooth surfaces and proper fit
Inspect main frame components for manufacturing defects and ensure hole alignment
Pre-assemble mechanical joints and test fit to verify clearances
Solder XT60 connector to Main LiPo Battery (if not pre-attached) and to Power Distribution Board
Solder motor phase wires from each ESC to its corresponding motor
Solder power input wires from Power Distribution Board to each ESC
Connect power output (5V, 12V) from Power Distribution Board to Flight Controller, Payload MCU, GNSS Module, Telemetry Radio, Magnetometers, and Laser Altimeter
Connect PWM signal wires from Flight Controller to each ESC
Wire all UART/I2C/RS485 data connections between Flight Controller, Payload MCU, GNSS Module, Telemetry Radio, Magnetometers, and Laser Altimeter
Perform continuity checks on all power and data lines to prevent shorts
Connect Flight Controller to computer, flash drone firmware, and perform initial configuration
Connect Payload MCU to computer, upload custom sensor acquisition and processing code
Power on the system (without propellers), verify 5V and 12V power rails with multimeter
Verify GNSS module is recognized by Flight Controller and receiving satellite data
Test Telemetry Radio communication between drone and ground station
Verify data acquisition from magnetometers and laser altimeter via Payload MCU serial console
Calibrate ESCs and perform individual motor spin tests using flight controller software (without propellers)
Assemble the main frame by attaching arms to the bottom plate and then securing the top plate with standoffs and screws
Mount ESCs into their respective 3D printed mounts and attach mounts to the main frame bottom plate
Mount motors to the frame arms and attach propellers, ensuring correct rotation direction and tight fit
Mount the Flight Controller and Payload MCU to their 3D printed mounts and secure them to the main frame bottom plate using nylon standoffs/screws
Mount the Boom Retraction Mechanism to the bottom plate and attach the Collapsible Sensor Boom, then mount magnetometers and laser altimeter to boom mounts
Assemble the GNSS mast and mount the GNSS module to its top mount, then attach the mast to the main frame top plate
Mount the Telemetry Radio into its 3D printed mount and secure it to the main frame bottom plate
Route all cables neatly, use cable ties for strain relief, and secure the Main LiPo Battery with the battery strap

projectDescription: This quadcopter drone employs two triaxial fluxgate magnetometers on a retractable boom to detect unexploded ordnance. It uses centimeter-level GPS for precise mapping and a laser altimeter for autonomous, low-altitude terrain following. The non-magnetic frame minimizes interference for accurate magnetic anomaly detection.

Deliverables required from Blueprints.am:
1. Complete BOM (Bill of Materials) with component specifications and estimated costs.
2. Circuit schematic diagram for sensor integration (Arduino + magnetometers + GPS + telemetry).
3. 3D CAD model of the drone frame with sensor boom mounting.
4. Step-by-step assembly and calibration instructions.
5. Flight parameters (PIDs, waypoint planning for grid scanning).

Design notes: Quadcopter type, Max 1.5 kg MTOW, 15 min endurance, 0.5-1.0 m AGL flight, Terrain following, Non-magnetic frame, Retractable boom, Centimeter-level GPS

Electrical components (current): 2x mcu: Flight Controller, Payload Data Acquisition MCU; 4x sensor: Centimeter-Level GPS Module, Vertical Magnetometer (Z-axis), Horizontal Magnetometer (X-axis), Precision Laser Altimeter; 2x power: Power Distribution Board with BECs, Main LiPo Battery; 8x actuator: Motor Front Left, Motor Front Right, Motor Rear Left, Motor Rear Right +4 more; Telemetry Radio Module (module, 50x25x10mm)

Mechanical/structural parts (current): 7x structural: Main Frame Bottom Plate, Main Frame Top Plate, Frame Arm Front Left, Frame Arm Front Right +3 more; 5x mechanism: Propeller Front Left, Propeller Front Right, Propeller Rear Left, Propeller Rear Right +1 more; 13x 3d_printed: Boom Retraction Mechanism, Z-Axis Magnetometer Mount, X-Axis Magnetometer Mount, Laser Altimeter Mount +9 more; 5x misc: LiPo Battery Strap, M3 Nylon Standoff, M3 Nylon Screw, M3 Nylon Nut +1 more

Assembly: Main Frame Bottom Plate → Main Frame Top Plate (M3 nylon standoffs and screws); Main Frame Bottom Plate → Frame Arm Front Left (M3 bolts); Main Frame Bottom Plate → Frame Arm Front Right (M3 bolts); Main Frame Bottom Plate → Frame Arm Rear Left (M3 bolts); Main Frame Bottom Plate → Frame Arm Rear Right (M3 bolts); Main Frame Bottom Plate → Landing Skid Set (M3 bolts); Frame Arm Front Left → Propeller Front Left (M3 bolts); Frame Arm Front Right → Propeller Front Right (M3 bolts); Frame Arm Rear Left → Propeller Rear Left (M3 bolts); Frame Arm Rear Right → Propeller Rear Right (M3 bolts); Main Frame Bottom Plate → Boom Retraction Mechanism (M3 bolts); Boom Retraction Mechanism → Collapsible Sensor Boom (integrated mounting); Collapsible Sensor Boom → Z-Axis Magnetometer Mount (M2 screws); Collapsible Sensor Boom → X-Axis Magnetometer Mount (M2 screws); Collapsible Sensor Boom → Laser Altimeter Mount (M2 screws) (+27 more connections)

assumptions: Familiarity with drone flight controller software (e.g., ArduPilot, PX4) setup and flashing
Experience with programming microcontrollers (e.g., Arduino IDE for Payload MCU)
Understanding of LiPo battery safety and handling
Access to a computer with necessary drivers and software for component configuration
Knowledge of basic serial communication protocols (UART, I2C, RS485)
